smoke.detail: OPERA 2.9 + MATLAB MCR v912 installed on the box under /zfs/sanjanp/fto-admet-envs/opera (6.5G); fixed OPERA_installdir.txt to the /zfs path, then ran run_OPERA.sh on the FTO-43 fixture with -e LogP LogD pKa FuB Clint Caco2 -> a real preds.csv (the _predRange build, with LogD55/LogD74 + ionization columns). The committed parser produced 8 OutputRecords, ALL valid against core.schemas.OutputRecord: LogP=-0.17 (conf 0.966, AD in), pKa_a=3.95, LogD(pH7.4)=-3.62 (conf 0.975, AD in), FuB=0.88 (conf 0.948), Clint=4.49 (AD out), Caco2=NaN. Parser now canonicalizes LogD74->LogD, FUB->FuB, CACO2->Caco2 so OPERA feeds the lipophilicity/ppb aggregators, and the shared AD_LogD/Conf_index_LogD reattach to the LogD prediction.

note: OPERA runtime (MATLAB MCR v912 + Java PaDEL/CDK) installed on the box under /zfs and run for real on the fixture; the synthetic sample is superseded by a real preds.csv. Parser gained a canonical-endpoint map verified against the real header so downstream aggregators actually receive OPERA's LogD/FuB/Caco2 lenses. Out-of-band model-heavy: README recipe is the contract, no in-folder pixi lock.
